# Scope Management Plan

## Purpose
Define how project scope is defined, validated, and controlled.

## Audience
Project Manager, Business Analyst, PMO, Sponsors.

## Inputs
- Project charter (`docs/templates/governance/project-charter-template-waterfall.md`).
- WBS (`docs/templates/schedule/wbs-template-waterfall.yaml`).

## 1. Scope Statement Definition Process
Describe how scope is documented, reviewed, and approved. Reference the project scope statement
template (`docs/templates/requirements/project-scope-statement-template-cross.md`) and include:
- Inputs (business case, charter, BRD/SRS, stakeholder interviews).
- Review cadence and sign-off criteria.
- Baseline versioning and document control.

## 2. WBS Development & Maintenance
Describe WBS decomposition rules, versioning, and dictionary ownership. Include:
- Decomposition approach (deliverable-based, phase-based, or hybrid).
- Work package definition standards and acceptance criteria.
- WBS dictionary maintenance and approval workflow.

## 3. Scope Change Process
Reference change request workflow and baseline updates. Include:
- Intake requirements and change requests.
- Impact analysis expectations (schedule, cost, resources, risk).
- Decision authority (CCB, sponsor, PMO) and approval SLAs.
- Traceability updates (RTM, scope baseline, schedule baseline).

## 4. Validation & Acceptance Criteria
Define acceptance criteria and sign-off procedures for deliverables. Include:
- Verification methods (inspection, demonstration, testing, analysis).
- Acceptance checkpoints tied to milestones.
- Documentation required for acceptance (test reports, RTM updates).

## 5. Roles & Responsibilities
| Role | Responsibilities | Approval Authority |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

## 6. Tools & Techniques
List tools used for scope tracking, requirements management, and traceability (e.g., RTM,
requirements repository, WBS management tool, change log).

## 7. Scope Reporting & Metrics
Define how scope health is reported and tracked. Include:
- Scope performance indicators (requirements completeness, change volume).
- Reporting cadence and audience.
- Escalation path for scope risks or uncontrolled changes.

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Scope statement and WBS baseline approved.
- [ ] Change control process documented.
- [ ] Acceptance criteria defined for deliverables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scope management plan aligns with charter and stakeholder expectations.
- Scope change control is documented and enforceable.
